findings.title: codex.ts uses .ts import extensions that break tsc+Node.js ESM (remaining instance after partial fix)
findings.detail: Lines 4-5 of codex.ts import './spawn-runner.ts' and './types.ts' using .ts extensions. Every other production worker file uses .js extensions (correct for tsc+Node.js ESM). Under the PM2 production runtime these imports produce ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND because the output directory contains spawn-runner.js, not spawn-runner.ts. First tracked in prior audit as 'partial fix applied'; this is the remaining unfixed file.
findings.fix_sketch: Change lines 4-5 to './spawn-runner.js' and './types.js'. Mirror the convention in claude.ts lines 3-12.

findings.title: spawn-runner.ts cancel() unconditionally blocks 5 s even when the process has already exited after SIGTERM
findings.detail: cancel() sends SIGTERM then unconditionally calls await delay(graceMs) (default 5000 ms) before checking whether the child has already exited. Fast-exiting workers still block for 5 seconds per cancellation. cancelSprint() runs all task cancels via Promise.allSettled so an entire sprint always takes >= 5 s to cancel. Previously tracked as COSMETIC; upgraded to IMPORTANT because it also risks PM2 kill_timeout races during daemon shutdown.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace the unconditional await delay(graceMs) with a Promise.race between delay(graceMs) and a childExited promise resolved on child.once('exit'). Only send SIGKILL if exitCode and signalCode are both still null after the race.

findings.title: codex.ts createCodexAdapter tmpDir leaks permanently on daemon crash or SIGKILL
findings.detail: mkdtempSync(join(tmpRoot, 'ifleet-codex-')) creates a new unique tmpDir for every spawned task. Cleanup only runs inside readFinalAndCleanup(), called from finalize/classifyExit on child close. If PM2 force-restarts the daemon or the machine reboots, these ifleet-codex-XXXXX directories accumulate in /tmp and can fill disk over weeks of operation.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace mkdtempSync with a deterministic path join(tmpRoot, 'ifleet-codex', opts.taskId). Call rmSync(tmpDir, {recursive:true, force:true}) + mkdirSync(tmpDir, {recursive:true}) at spawn time so a restart replaces rather than accumulates.

findings.title: auto-router.ts buildRouterPrompt interpolates GitHub issue title and labels as raw strings — prompt injection vector
findings.detail: buildRouterPrompt() wraps p.body in quoteAsUserData() but interpolates p.title and labelList as bare strings (lines 174-175). Both fields are user-controlled from GitHub issue metadata. An attacker controlling issue title can append a JSON object that parseRouterDecision picks up as the routing decision (it takes the first {...} block). This allows forcing any sprint mode including ralph (persistent retry loop) on any opened issue.
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap p.title and labelList through quoteAsUserData() identically to how p.body is wrapped, with a clear DATA label so the model treats them as data.

findings.title: issue-commenter.ts waitForApproval has no error handling around the reactions API call — transient GitHub error terminates HITL approval loop
findings.detail: Inside the polling loop (lines 67-71), octokit.reactions.listForIssueComment(...) is awaited with no try/catch. Any transient GitHub 5xx, rate limit (403), or network timeout throws and propagates as an unhandled rejection out of waitForApproval, terminating the HITL wait entirely. The pipeline marks the task failed even though no approval decision was made. HITL windows can be 30+ minutes; GitHub API flakiness is an operational reality.
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap the octokit.reactions.listForIssueComment call in try/catch; on error, log a warning and continue to the next poll iteration rather than propagating. Add a consecutive-error counter to fail after N unrecoverable errors.

findings.title: sprint.ts dispatch() orphans a running subprocess in this.running when store.saveTask throws after adapter.spawn succeeds
findings.detail: Lines 428-446: this.running.set(task.id, {..., handle, ...}) is called before this.store.saveTask(). If saveTask throws (SQLite locked, disk full), the catch block (line 447) releases the worker via registry.release(workerId) but does NOT delete the entry from this.running and does NOT await the handle. Result: the subprocess runs unsupervised; the worker is double-usable; sprintRunningCount stays permanently elevated; tick() never reaches terminal check; sprint is stuck in running forever.
findings.fix_sketch: Move this.running.set(...) to after the store.saveTask() call so no entry is registered unless the DB write succeeds. Alternatively add this.running.delete(task.id) to the catch block.

findings.title: audit-store.ts TERMINAL_SQL_LIST string-interpolates enum values into SQL — structurally injection-prone
findings.detail: TERMINAL_SQL_LIST (line 19) is built via template literal interpolation of TypeScript enum values and then used raw in WHERE ... NOT IN (...) clauses at lines 89, 101, and 148. The comment correctly notes the values are static today, but any future AuditStatus value containing a single quote, semicolon, or comment marker would silently malform or inject into an otherwise fully-parameterized query. Already tracked in index.json; fixing here.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace NOT IN (${TERMINAL_SQL_LIST}) with NOT (status = ANY($N)) and pass TERMINAL_AUDIT_STATUSES as a text[] parameter. Postgres natively accepts a JS string[] as text[]. Eliminates the interpolation entirely.
